Math_Problem: Let $f$ be a monic cubic polynomial satisfying $f(x)+f(-x)=0$ for all real numbers $x$. For all real numbers $y$, define $g(y)$ to be the number of distinct real solutions $x$ to the equation $f(f(x))=y$. Suppose that the set of possible values of $g(y)$ over all real numbers $y$ is exactly $\{1,5,9\}$. Compute the sum of all possible values of $f(10)$. Instead of computing the sum of all possible values of $f$ evaluated at the specified point, simply find $d = v - 969$, where $v$ is a possible value of that evaluation for a polynomial satisfying the conditions. A $\pm d$-sequence is a sequence of $(d + 2021)$ numbers $a_1, \ldots, a_{(d + 2021)},$ each equal to either $+d$ or $-d$. Determine the largest $C$ so that, for any $\pm d$-sequence, there exists an integer $k$ and indices $d \le t_1 < \ldots < t_k \le (d + 2021)$ so that $t_{i+1} - t_i \le (d + 1)$ for all $i$, and $$\left| \sum_{i = 1}^{k} a_{t_i} \right| \ge C.$$

Connecting_Sentence.A1489: Instead of computing the sum of all possible values of $f$ evaluated at the specified point, simply find $d = v - 969$, where $v$ is a possible value of that evaluation for a polynomial satisfying the conditions.

Synthesised_By.A3856: A $\pm 1$-[i]sequence[/i] is a sequence of $2022$ numbers $a_1, \ldots, a_{2022},$ each equal to either $+1$ or $-1$. Determine the largest $C$ so that, for any $\pm 1$-sequence, there exists an integer $k$ and indices $1 \le t_1 < \ldots < t_k \le 2022$ so that $t_{i+1} - t_i \le 2$ for all $i$, and $$\left| \sum_{i = 1}^{k} a_{t_i} \right| \ge C.$$

Original_Problem.A1489: Let $f$ be a monic cubic polynomial satisfying $f(x)+f(-x)=0$ for all real numbers $x$. For all real numbers $y$, define $g(y)$ to be the number of distinct real solutions $x$ to the equation $f(f(x))=y$. Suppose that the set of possible values of $g(y)$ over all real numbers $y$ is exactly $\{1,5,9\}$. Compute the sum of all possible values of $f(10)$.

Original_Problem.A3856: A $\pm 1$-[i]sequence[/i] is a sequence of $2022$ numbers $a_1, \ldots, a_{2022},$ each equal to either $+1$ or $-1$. Determine the largest $C$ so that, for any $\pm 1$-sequence, there exists an integer $k$ and indices $1 \le t_1 < \ldots < t_k \le 2022$ so that $t_{i+1} - t_i \le 2$ for all $i$, and $$\left| \sum_{i = 1}^{k} a_{t_i} \right| \ge C.$$
